hints.Conjunction: Conjunctions join clauses (et, sed) — 'in' governs a noun
hints.Interjection: Interjections are exclamations — 'in' takes an object

hints.Accusative: 'in' + accusative means motion toward — this is location ('in the highest'), not motion

hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'Gloria' would be 'Gloria' (same form), but here it is the subject

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'Gloria' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'Gloria' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'Gloria' is a typical 1st declension -a noun

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural neuter would be 'excelsa' — 'excelsis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.Dative: 'excelsis' could be dative plural, but governed by 'in' it must be ablative

hints.Ablative: 'Deo' could be ablative, but here it means 'to God' — glory is given to God (dative)

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'Deus' follows 2nd declension -us pattern
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied nominatives — 'Deus' with genitive 'Dei' is 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension -us nouns have genitive -us (e.g. 'manus, manus') — 'Dei' is 2nd declension

translation: Glory to God in the highest
